mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 220.0, real interest rates turn deeply negative, triggering expectations of aggressive central bank tightening that compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.0, or the threshold signal shows no statistical separation from baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and decline in total nonfarm payrolls confirms labor market deterioration, signaling imminent corporate earnings contraction that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint labor deterioration periods yield positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated retail sales and housing starts indicate robust consumer spending that supports near-term corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days during late-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when applied outside late-cycle expansion, or 5-day returns are negative in more than 60% of high-demand observations.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boosts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO momentum (t-stat < 1.5) across full sample periods, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth falls below 0.5%, aggregate demand contraction triggers recession risk pricing that suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 70% of low-GDP-growth quarters, or the signal fails to separate recessionary from expansionary periods in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the federal funds rate and decreases in inflation confirm a successful disinflation trajectory that preserves real yields while avoiding recession, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction periods produce negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models in cross-validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rates during historically tight labor markets signal structural labor market breakdown that forces corporate cost-cutting and earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising unemployment in tight labor regimes yields positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the regime-conditional effect is indistinguishable from baseline unemployment trends.
